claim: Skill-grounded scoring shows better aggregate inter-judge consistency than generic-rubric scoring in the reported comparison.
scope: 92 Figma runs reported in rubric comparison
caveat: Comparison changed author source rubric count grouping weights anchor language and observability together
decision_use: Supporting: motivates a controlled pilot, but cannot attribute the difference specifically to skill grounding.

claim: The generic-rubric comparator had lower reported aggregate agreement.
caveat: Does not isolate expert authorship as the cause
decision_use: Supporting comparator evidence for a pilot; not a causal basis for adoption.

claim: Expert-rubric scores had lower reported judge variance.
decision_use: Supporting evidence for score consistency while explicitly not establishing validity.

claim: The generic-rubric comparator had higher reported judge variance.
decision_use: Supporting comparator evidence, limited to consistency rather than validity.

claim: Automated output grades do not yet track individual human judgments well enough to support unqualified adoption.
caveat: One Figma domain expert limits human-baseline generality
decision_use: Disconfirming: weak individual-level human/LLM concordance requires human oversight and prospective validation.

claim: Automated skill grades lack demonstrated individual-level human concordance and independent process validation.
caveat: No independent human process annotation validates the skill judges
decision_use: Disconfirming: blocks treating skill scores as validated professional judgments.

claim: The configured-system ablation is directional and heterogeneous rather than a stable general effect.
scope: Claude Codex and Gemini harness/model systems
caveat: Sample is underpowered and harness/model/integration factors remain bundled
decision_use: Mixed: justifies further controlled testing but not immediate adoption or a general causal claim.

claim: One configured-system subset showed a large directional gain.
scope: Subset of the 7 paired Figma runs
caveat: Too few runs for a stable causal adoption claim
decision_use: Supporting for a pilot only; the estimate is too unstable for adoption.

claim: Some no-skill runs deployed, so deployment outcomes do not isolate the skill procedure's effect.
caveat: Deployment difference alone cannot separate procedure content from integration and stochasticity
decision_use: Disconfirming for causal adoption; informs the need to control integration and stochasticity.

claim: Pairwise preference did not consistently establish absolute professional readiness.
scope: 31 rated Figma tasks with one domain expert
caveat: Single-expert threshold is not a universal professional standard
decision_use: Disconfirming: adoption requires a separately validated absolute-readiness gate.
